volumeTitle: Persians. Seven against Thebes. Suppliants. Prometheus Bound
label: Seven against Thebes (Section 1060 - Section 1080)

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.translation: O Erinyes , far-famed destroyers of families , goddesses of death who have thus laid ruin to the family of Oedipus , digging it up from the roots ! What will happen to me ? What should I do ? What plan shall I devise ? How can I have the heart neither to weep for you

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.translation: nor escort you to your tomb ? But I am afraid and turn away in terror of the citizens . You , at least , Eteocles , will have many mourners , while he , wretched man , departs without lamentation

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: εἶσιν : τίς ἂν οὖν τὰ πίθοιτο ; [*]
sections.translation: and has a dirge sung only by one sister . Now who could comply with that ?

sections.label: Section 1072
sections.translation: Let the city take action or not take action against those who lament for Polynices . We , at all events , will go and

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: καὶ γὰρ γενεᾷ κοινὸν τόδ ἄχος ,
sections.translation: bury him with her , following the funeral procession . For this grief is shared by all our race , and the city approves as just different things at different times .

sections.label: Section 1078
sections.original: ἡμεῖς δ ἅμα τῷδ , ὥσπερ τε πόλις
sections.translation: We will go with this other corpse , as the city and justice , too , approves .

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: μετὰ γὰρ μάκαρας καὶ Διὸς ἰσχὺν
sections.translation: For after the blessed gods and powerful Zeus , he it was who saved the city of the Cadmeans from being capsized and flooded by a wave of foreign men — he beyond all others . Exeunt omnes .
